trl_rationale: Academic sources describe only conceptual designs and early-stage research for solid tumors (A2, A5), with no approved products or clinical trial results in the registry. Market sources confirm no commercial products exist (M2, M3, M4), and only one early-phase clinical trial is identified (M4). Thus, the technology is at laboratory validation stage.
mrl_rationale: Academic sources discuss manufacturing challenges and propose strategies (A5, M5), but no pilot-scale or production-line evidence is provided. Market sources mention a collaboration to automate manufacturing (M1) but no operational production. Manufacturing remains at lab-scale proof-of-concept.
patent_rationale: Patents cover key strategies (e.g., hyaluronidase, PD-1 knockout, pan-solid tumor targeting) but leave white spaces in combination therapies and manufacturing innovations (PF4, PF5). The landscape is moderately contested with opportunities for new entrants.
market_rationale: No commercial products exist for solid tumors; only early-stage clinical trials and research collaborations are identified (M1, M4). Market reports project growth but do not break out solid tumor segment (M6). Commercial activity is at early R&D stage.
evidence_rationale: All three domains (academic, patent, market) are covered with credible sources, but academic sources are limited (only 2 directly address solid tumors), patent landscape is based on 6 patents, and market sources lack independent revenue data. Moderate confidence with minor gaps.

key_risks: No approved CAR-T products for solid tumors; clinical efficacy unproven (A2, A5)
Manufacturing scalability and cost remain major hurdles (M5)
Patent landscape may have blocking patents from major players not captured in registry
Market size projections are for entire CAR-T market; solid tumor segment uncertain (M6)
key_opportunities: White space for combination therapies (e.g., checkpoint inhibitors) not yet patented (PF4)
Manufacturing automation and point-of-care production could reduce costs and improve access (M1)
Novel targeting strategies (e.g., pan-solid tumor antigens) offer differentiation (P4)
Early-phase clinical trials (NCT03618381) may validate new targets and attract investment (M4)
